methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
DroneShield is a public-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $100M+. The valuation universe consists of 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI, drawn from a broader Defense & Security sector with 21 identified peers.

The peer-median triangulation method proved inapplicable to this analysis. The Defense & Security sector contains 21 same-sector peers, but none satisfied the narrow filtering criteria required for reliable peer-median valuation—specifically, no three or more comparables met both stage-alignment and valuation-tier similarity thresholds simultaneously. The same-stage and adjacent-stage filters yielded zero qualifying datapoints, making an all-sector median approach methodologically unreliable and potentially misleading given the heterogeneity of Defense & Security subsegments.

Given the failure of primary triangulation, valuation relies on qualitative assessment of the identified public and private comp universe. The 2 public peers and 8 private references provide directional guidance on market pricing within the sector, though without quantitative multiples or transaction data, no specific valuation range can be supported. Key uncertainties include DroneShield's relative margin profile versus public comparables, the applicability of private-market growth multiples to a public entity, and the absence of recent transaction data in the narrow Defense & Security frontier-tech segment. Additional comps or disclosed financial metrics would be required to triangulate a defensible valuation range.
